flashcards.front: What are the four main components of the Von Neumann Architecture?
flashcards.back: 1. Central Processing Unit (CPU), 2. Memory Unit (MU), 3. Input/Output (I/O) System, 4. System Bus.

flashcards.front: What is the role of the Control Unit (CU)?
flashcards.back: The CU is the coordinator of the CPU. It fetches instructions from memory, decodes them, and generates the control signals needed to execute them by directing the ALU, registers, and memory.

flashcards.front: What is the difference between the MAR and the MDR?
flashcards.back: The Memory Address Register (MAR) holds the address of the memory location to be accessed. The Memory Data Register (MDR), also called Memory Buffer Register (MBR), holds the actual data that is being read from or written to that address.

flashcards.front: List the three primary steps of the instruction cycle.
What are the three sub-buses of a system bus and their purposes?
flashcards.back: 1. Data Bus: Transfers data/instructions. 2. Address Bus: Carries memory addresses. 3. Control Bus: Carries timing and control signals (e.g., Read, Write).
